pi_items.source: Code analysis: setup_task.sh (line 60 removes this file), export_result.sh (line 24 expects this filename), verifier.py (line 47 checks drawio_exists). All three sources confirm this filename.
Code analysis: setup_task.sh (line 61 removes this file), export_result.sh (line 25 expects this filename), verifier.py (line 94 checks png_exists). All sources confirm.

Code analysis reveals mismatch: export_result.sh (lines 78-82) defines the actual keywords the verifier checks, which differ significantly from metadata claims. Verifier checks 7 keywords and requires 4+ matches for full credit (verifier.py line 64). Metadata list omits section headers (Goals, Frustrations) but includes personality dimensions (Analytical, Creative) that verifier ignores.

Code analysis: Task description specifies 3 personality sliders (Introvert-Extrovert, Analytical-Creative, Tech-Savvy), each requiring a line for the track. Verifier checks for circles >= 3 AND lines >= 3 (export_result.sh lines 110-111, verifier.py line 77).

Code analysis: Task description specifies 3 personality sliders each with a circle (ellipse) for the handle. Verifier explicitly counts ellipses and checks for circles >= 3 (export_result.sh lines 95-97, verifier.py line 77).

privileged_info_summary: Task uses a synthetic persona (Penny Parker) with auto-generated profile photo and hardcoded text data created by setup_task.sh. Expected output filenames are persona_card.drawio and persona_card.png. Personality sliders require minimum 3 lines and 3 circles. CRITICAL: required_text claim in metadata is contradicted by verifier—metadata lists [Penny Parker, Senior Project Manager, Introvert, Extrovert, Analytical, Creative] but verifier actually checks for [Penny, Parker, Project Manager, Introvert, Extrovert, Goals, Frustrations], omitting personality trait names (Analytical, Creative) while requiring section headers (Goals, Frustrations) not listed in metadata.
